unit_breakdown_theory.unit: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques

rules: No chapter wise weightage is provided; care to be taken to cover all chapters.
Suitable internal variations may be made for generating various templates.
There will be no overall choice in the question paper.
33% internal choices will be given in all the sections.

formative_only_topics: s & p Block Elements (Electronic configuration, atomic & Ionic radii, Ionization Enthalpy, Hydration Enthalpy and general trends)
competency_weightage.note: Topics included in syllabus but assessed only formatively to reinforce understanding without adding to summative assessments.

formative_only_topics: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Elements
competency_weightage.note: Topics included in syllabus but assessed only formatively to reinforce understanding without adding to summative assessments.

bloom_levels.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations. Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas or quality of work based on a set of criteria. Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

written_test_details.type: Practical skill based very short answer type questions

experiments: Cutting glass tube and glass rod
Bending a glass tube
Drawing out a glass jet
Boring a cork

syllabus_categories.category: Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances

experiments.experiment: Determination of melting point of an organic compound
Determination of boiling point of an organic compound

experiments: Determination of pH of solutions from fruit juices/known concentrations using pH paper/universal indicator
Comparing the pH of strong and weak acids of same concentration
Study pH change in titration of a strong base using universal indicator
Study pH change by common-ion effect
Shift in equilibrium between ferric ions and thiocyanate ions
Shift in equilibrium between [Co(H2O)6]2+ and chloride ions
Using a mechanical/electronic balance
Preparation of standard solution of Oxalic acid
Determination of strength of NaOH by titration against standard Oxalic acid
Preparation of standard solution of Sodium carbonate
Determination of strength of HCl by titration against standard Sodium Carbonate
Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt (Cations: Pb, Cu, Al, Fe, Mn, Ni, Zn, Co, Ca, Sr, Ba, Mg, NH4+; Anions: CO3, S, SO3, NO3, NO2, Cl, Br, I, SO4, PO4, CH3COO)
Detection of Nitrogen, Sulphur, Chlorine in organic compounds
Crystallization (copper sulphate, benzoic acid)
Determination of pH using pH paper
Comparing pH of strong/weak acids
Shift in equilibrium (ferric/thiocyanate)
Shift in equilibrium (Co complex)
Preparation of standard oxalic acid
Determination of molarity of NaOH by titration against oxalic acid
One anion and one cation (NH4+, CO3, S, SO3, Cl, CH3COO)
Detection of Nitrogen in organic compound
Detection of Halogen in organic compound
Preparation of lyophilic sol (starch, egg albumin, gum) and lyophobic sol (aluminum hydroxide, ferric hydroxide, arsenous sulphide)
Dialysis of sols prepared above
Study role of emulsifying agents in stabilizing emulsion
Effect of concentration and temperature on rate between Sodium Thiosulphate and Hydrochloric acid
Study reaction rates (Iodide ion + H2O2 OR KIO3 + Na2SO3 with starch indicator)
Enthalpy of dissolution (Copper Sulphate or Potassium Nitrate)
Enthalpy of neutralization (HCl and NaOH)
Determination of enthalpy change during interaction between Acetone and Chloroform
Variation of cell potential in Zn/Zn || Cu/Cu with concentration changes (CuSO4 or ZnSO4)
Separation of pigments from leaves/flowers and determination of Rf values
Separation of constituents in an inorganic mixture containing two cations
Double salt (Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate or Potash Alum)
Potassium Ferric Oxalate
Acetanilide
Di-benzal acetone
p-Nitroacetanilide
Aniline yellow or 2-Naphthol Aniline dye
Unsaturation, alcoholic, phenolic, aldehydic, ketonic, carboxylic and amino (Primary) groups
Tests for carbohydrates, fats and proteins in pure samples and foodstuffs
Determination of concentration/molarity of KMnO4 solution by titrating against standard Oxalic acid or Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate
Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt (Cations: Pb, Cu, Al, Fe, Mn, Ni, Zn, Co, Ca, Sr, Ba, Mg, NH4+; Anions: CO3, S, SO3, NO3, NO2, Cl, Br, I, SO4, PO4, CH3COO, C2O4)
Preparation of lyophilic and lyophobic sols
Separation of pigments from leaves/flowers (Rf values)
Alcoholic, Carboxylic groups; Aldehyde, Ketonic groups
Carbohydrates and proteins in foodstuffs

project_assessment.description: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.
suggested_projects: Checking the bacterial contamination in drinking water by testing sulphide ion
Study of the methods of purification of water
Testing hardness/presence of Iron, Fluoride, Chloride etc. in drinking water and study causes
Investigation of foaming capacity of different washing soaps and effect of Sodium carbonate
Study acidity of different samples of tea leaves
Determination of rate of evaporation of different liquids
Effect of acids/bases on tensile strength of fibers
Acidity of fruit and vegetable juices

project_assessment.description: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.
suggested_projects: Study presence of oxalate ions in guava fruit at different stages of ripening
Quantity of casein present in different samples of milk
Preparation of soybean milk and comparison with natural milk (curd formation, temperature effect)
Effect of Potassium Bisulphate as food preservative under various conditions
Digestion of starch by salivary amylase and effect of pH/temperature
Comparative study of rate of fermentation (wheat flour, gram flour, potato juice, carrot juice)
Extraction of essential oils from Saunf, Ajwain, Illaichi
Study common food adulterants in fat, oil, butter, sugar, turmeric powder, chili powder and pepper
